page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, utilizing dedicated CPU allocation. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 25%, and average single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory subsystem performance is also constrained, with cached read-only memory bandwidth at 126.7 GB/sec and uncached write bandwidth at 20.4 GB/sec, both ranking in the lower tiers of tested hardware.

Given its hardware profile, the n2d-standard-2 is suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads that do not demand high parallel processing or intensive memory throughput. It is appropriate for basic web serving, text processing, and small-scale application hosting. However, it is poorly suited for database operations, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or real-time ray tracing due to low multi-core and database benchmark scores. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a low-entry resource footprint for entry-level dedicated compute needs, though users requiring high memory bandwidth or storage-heavy operations will face performance bottlenecks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is an entry-level general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It utilizes a dedicated CPU allocation with 1 physical core and 2 threads. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, alongside average single-threaded compression and text processing speeds. However, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations are relatively weak, placing in the lower percentiles. Consequently, the n2d-standard-2 is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic web serving, and small-scale development tasks that do not require intensive parallel processing or high memory throughput.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a 2-vCPU, 8 GB RAM general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with dedicated CPU allocation.
tagline: An entry-level dedicated compute instance featuring strong single-core performance and balanced memory for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Dedicated CPU allocation with 1 physical core and 2 threads
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic development
